mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for deploying large language models efficiently and reliably in production environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for adequate hardware resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface, which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary for Linux.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create the Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count in the desired region.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to use the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to point to the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d Helm chart into the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Adds the llm-d Helm repository.
> Installs llm-d in its own namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for llm-d.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaling configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes llm-d from your cluster and warns about potential data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

kubectl delete namespace llm-d

```

> This command removes the llm-d release.
> This command deletes the llm-d namespace, which may result in data loss.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up your configuration, upgrading the Helm chart, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup current configuration.
> Upgrades llm-d to the specified version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1' to refresh kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the application inside the pod is failing to start.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n llm-d' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the llm-d deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize the nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Service not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the service you are trying to access is not deployed or has been deleted.

**Fix:**
Verify that the service is running with 'kubectl get services -n llm-d'.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for dynamic multi-workload autoscaling and improved inference scheduling capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d
helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
